title: When question comes in `chatUserActionRequired` signal does not play

- Use `#askQuestions`
- 🐛 no `chatUserActionRequired` sound plays

title: PromptFileParser fails to parse most of the `Explore` agent prompt file
body:
Only 2 headers are detected after parsing (name and description)

The agent has a target of `vscode`
As the parser fails, this agent is displayed for all chat types

Also, i don't think the right tools would have been used either

title: tool_search_tool_regex enters retry loop when MCP server fails to activate
body:
When an MCP server fails to activate (e.g. server is unavailable or misconfigured), the model repeatedly calls `tool_search_tool_regex` in an infinite loop trying to load the deferred MCP tools. Each attempt fails, but the model keeps retrying with the same or similar queries, never giving up.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Configure an MCP server in `.vscode/mcp.json` that fails to activate (e.g. unreachable URL)
2. Ask the model to use a tool from that MCP server (e.g. "create a GitHub issue")
3. Observe the model calling `tool_search_tool_regex` repeatedly without stopping

**Expected:** The model should recognize after 1-2 failed attempts that the tool is unavailable and inform the user, rather than looping indefinitely.

**Actual:** The model calls `tool_search_tool_regex` in a loop, wasting tokens and never completing the request.

title: Tips toolbar: Dismissed tips are not staying dismissed when feature is re-enabled
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

**Describe the bug**
When a user dismisses a tip in the tips toolbar, it should not appear again for the same user, even after re-enabling the "chat.tips.enabled" setting. However, after re-enabling, dismissed tips repeatedly come back when clicking the checkmark, rather than leaving the user in a tip-free state as expected. This appears to be a regression from previous behavior.

**To Reproduce**
1. Navigate to the tips toolbar and dismiss some tips by clicking the checkmark.
2. Disable tips from the toolbar or context menu.
3. Re-enable tips by toggling the "chat.tips.enabled" setting.
4. Tips previously dismissed keep reappearing despite being acknowledged before.

**Expected behavior**
Once a tip has been dismissed, it should not show up again for the user, regardless of toggling the tips feature on and off. Dismissal should be persistent per user.

**Additional context**
- Some users would prefer not to disable tips outright if they knew dismissed tips would never reappear. Persistent dismissal enables them to keep tips on for new content without being bothered by repeats.
- There was discussion about whether navigating tips should mark them as read/dismissed (answer: no).
- This was confirmed as a regression in the Slack thread.

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: [Please fill]
- OS Version: [Please fill]

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Follow dismissal and re-enable steps as described above.
2. Observe repeated display of previously dismissed tips, which should not occur.

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C1C2M1KEZ/p1771433472070599?thread_ts=1771433472.070599&cid=C1C2M1KEZ)

title: Support dismissing modal overlays by clicking outside
body: Currently, modal overlays for Settings and Extensions can only be dismissed via the Escape key or the small close button (×) in the top-right corner of the modal. I find it rather unintuitive that I cannot click outside the modal's bounds to dismiss it. Would be great if that could be supported, either as an option or by default. Basically, I'd like to be able to click the yellow area to dismiss the modal: label:workbench-modal-editor ### Version Version: 1.110.0-insider (user setup) Commit: 2001cfd88a7e89fca268e64c29acf0da84e93591 Date: 2026-02-17T17:11:03.424Z Electron: 39.6.0 ElectronBuildId: 13312042 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.0 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26220 cc: @bpasero

title: Chat tip shown when anonymous user exceeds quota and upgrade widget is visible
body:
Repro steps:

1. Launch a new VS Code instance.
2. Enable the setting: `chat.allowAnonymousAccess` to allow anonymous access to GitHub Copilot.
3. Send a bunch of requests until you hit the quota (at this point, the upgrade prompt should show up on top of the chat input box).
4. Observe: Above the chat input, an upgrade prompt widget appears https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/294620/changes
5. **Bug:** Currently, the chat tip might also be shown at the same time. It should not be displayed when the quota/upgrade widget is visible above the input.

Expected:
- When the quota is hit and the upgrade widget is displayed, chat tips should not be shown above the input, as the user cannot act on them.

Please investigate so these onboarding tips are suppressed when the upgrade/budget widget takes precedence.

(Reference: feedback thread in Standup.)

[View original Slack conversation](https://vscodeteam.slack.com/archives/C1C2M1KEZ/p1771013688355029?thread_ts=1771013688.355029&cid=C1C2M1KEZ)

title: Integrated Browser + Live Preview: opened side-by-side window should have "Lock group"
body: In the old experience, the browser is opened on the side with a Locked group. In the new experience with Integrated Browser, it's not opened with a locked group. The reason why a locked group is useful is so that newly opened editors don't open on the same group as the browser, even if the browser is focused. --- Old experience ("livePreview.useIntegratedBrowser": false) -- note the Locked Group icon: New experience ("livePreview.useIntegratedBrowser": true) -- note no Locked Group icon: --- Note for verification: Requires installing the Live Preview extension (https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode.live-server)

title: Recent changes causing Gemini Pro model to hallucinate
body: Recently (past two or three days) Gemini Pro models have been hallucinating tool calls rather than making edits directly. This seems to be changes to the system prompt or something- it probably needs a reminder to use the tools. It's yielded some comical results, though: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.6 - VS Code Version: 1.109.4 - OS Version: Windows 11 (VSCode in WSL) - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Gemini 3 Pro - Logs: See attached diagnostics & transcript [copilot_diagnostics.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25371248/copilot_diagnostics.md) Full Transcript (Note that I start with Claude 4.6 then transition to Gemini 3 Pro at the end): [transcript.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25371226/transcript.md) Steps to Reproduce: 1. Choose Gemini Pro as the model 2. Ask it to solve a problem 3. Within 2 to 3 iterations it begins hallucinating tool calls rather than calling directly. This seems pretty consistently reproducible right now, in various chats.

title: ctrl+shift+h still fails for toggling state of AskQuestion experience
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 
- OS Version: 

Steps to Reproduce:

Hi,

The issue with ctrl+shift+h continues to persist in the latest VSCode release.

I have a suggestion to resolve the issue. Change the Search in Files hotkey and add this when condition:

!inChatInput

In this case, if the chat window is in focus then it does not fire that specific keystroke. This resolved the issue for me.

The other solution may be to tie agent constants as a part of the when condition, similar to how the ctrl+shift+t hotkey is done for todos.

Jeff

title: Missing closing '>' in keybinding placeholder breaks accessible view navigation hint
body:
## Bug Description

In `src/vs/workbench/contrib/accessibility/browser/accessibleView.ts`, the `_navigationHint()` method (line 867) has a keybinding placeholder missing its closing `>` delimiter.

The `<keybinding:...>` syntax is a placeholder resolved by `resolveContentAndKeybindingItems()`. Because the first placeholder is missing its closing `>`, it is **not recognized** and the raw placeholder text is shown to the user instead of the resolved keybinding label.

### Code

```typescript
private _navigationHint(): string {
    return localize('accessibleViewNextPreviousHint',
        "Show the next item{0} or previous item{1}.",
        `<keybinding:${AccessibilityCommandId.ShowNext}`,      // MISSING closing >
        `<keybinding:${AccessibilityCommandId.ShowPrevious}>`   // correct
    );
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable a screen reader
2. Open the Accessible View (e.g. from terminal or chat)
3. Open Accessible View Help
4. Read the navigation hint text

## Expected Behavior

The hint should read something like: "Show the next item (Alt+N) or previous item (Alt+P)." with both keybindings resolved.

## Actual Behavior

The "Show Next" keybinding is displayed as raw placeholder text because the closing `>` is missing, while "Show Previous" is resolved correctly.

## Fix

One-character fix — add the missing `>`:

```diff
- `<keybinding:${AccessibilityCommandId.ShowNext}`
+ `<keybinding:${AccessibilityCommandId.ShowNext}>`
```

## Impact

This is an accessibility issue - screen reader users cannot see the correct keybinding for navigating to the next item in the accessible view.

Looks like this is somewhere in the copilot cli provider
